summary: Docker build process failed when attempting to download the Nydus binary using wget from GitHub. The wget command exited with code 1, preceded by network fetch failures with 'context canceled' errors when pulling container image layers from quay.io and other registries.
root_cause: wget command to download Nydus binary from GitHub failed with exit code 1: 'process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1'. Multiple preceding fetch operations also failed with 'context canceled' errors when retrieving image layers from quay.io.

evidence.quote: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Explicit command failure: the wget download of Nydus binary from GitHub failed, which is the direct cause of the build failure.

evidence.quote: error="failed to do request: Get \"https://cdn01.quay.io/quayio-production-s3/...\": context canceled"
evidence.reason: Network-level fetch failure with context cancellation, indicating transient network issues during layer retrieval.

evidence.quote: error="failed to do request: Get \"https://quay.io/v2/minio/minio/blobs/...\": context canceled"
evidence.reason: Additional context canceled error on quay.io fetch, reinforcing network connectivity issues during the build.

evidence.quote: level=error msg="/moby.buildkit.v1.Control/Solve returned error: rpc error: code = Canceled desc = context canceled"
evidence.reason: buildkit solver returned a context canceled error, indicating the build was terminated due to network or transient failures.

suggested_fix: Investigate network connectivity from the CI runner to external sources (GitHub, quay.io). Retry the build as this appears to be a transient network issue. If the problem persists, verify that the Nydus release version is available at the GitHub URL and that the CI runner has unrestricted internet access to download external dependencies during container builds.

summary: Test `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed due to repeated 502 errors from github.com when attempting to download a test package. The error shows HTTPSConnectionPool hitting max retries after receiving multiple 502 gateway error responses.
root_cause: Transient GitHub.com server errors (502 Bad Gateway) causing pip install to fail when downloading from https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip. The test was rerun 3 times but consistently encountered the same network error.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Explicit error message showing 502 gateway errors from github.com preventing package download
Test was rerun 3 times, all encountering the same network error, indicating a persistent transient issue
pip command exited with error code 1 due to the network failure

suggested_fix: Retry the CI job. This is a transient network failure from GitHub's servers (502 errors), not a code issue. If failures persist, consider adding longer retry delays or exponential backoff for network-dependent tests.

summary: Jest test assertion failure in test/config/get.ts. The test expects `pnpm config get` to return a registry URL without a trailing slash, but the actual output includes a trailing slash.
root_cause: The test assertion at test/config/get.ts:32 expects the registry URL 'https://my-org.registry.example.com' but receives 'https://my-org.registry.example.com/' with a trailing slash appended.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.quote:
FAIL test/config/get.ts (17.807 s)
  ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

    expect(received).toBe(expected) // Object.is equality

    Expected: "https://my-org.registry.example.com"
    Received: "https://my-org.registry.example.com/"
evidence.reason: Explicit test failure showing a string mismatch; the received value has a trailing slash that is not in the expected value.
The failing assertion code and line number confirm the exact point of failure in the test file.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Only one test failed out of 485; this is an isolated assertion failure, not a widespread test suite problem.

suggested_fix: Update the test expectation in test/config/get.ts:32 to expect the trailing slash: `.toBe('https://my-org.registry.example.com/')`, or verify that a recent code change introduced trailing slash normalization in registry URL handling and adjust the test or code accordingly.
